verbatim_text: It's what universities and schools do. You know it's not very efficient. And the way it works is I perform some actions, and you try and copy me. In particular, I might produce a string of words, and you might try and say, your brain, not you really, but. Your brain would say, "How do I change my connection strength, so. I might have said that word next too?". You're trying to get the knowledge from one system to another system by mimicking the outputs of the other system for the same inputs. You are not copying the weights across, you're not looking inside, you're just taking its overt behaviour. And by mimicking the overt behaviour, you internalise the same knowledge. It works, we actually use it for taking a big neural net and putting the knowledge into a small neural net, but it's very slow. With neural nets, you can give the whole probability distribution over words, and it's much faster. But with people, it's very slow, 'cause I see you say a particular word, the word you chose to say, and there aren't many bits in that, there's only a few bits per word. So it's only of the order of a hundred bits per sentence. And so even if we were communicating at the maximum possible rate, it would be only a hundred bits per sentence, that order.
